summary: Surveyed three accessibility categories that the prior VoiceOver + Dynamic Type work did not cover: Reduce Motion / Reduce Transparency / Increase Contrast / Differentiate Without Color (10 findings); Haptic / sensory feedback (6 findings); Switch Control / Voice Control input labels / accessibility rotors and custom actions (7 findings). All three categories classified `none` at the category level: no @Environment reads for any of the four motion/transparency/contrast/color settings; zero haptic API usage anywhere; zero accessibilityCustomActions / accessibilityRotor / accessibilityInputLabels usages. Wrote findings to automation/proofs/app-accessibility-survey/manifest.json with per-surface recommendations. Queued 5 follow-up fix slices (pri 76-82) covering swipeAction-to-accessibilityAction, reduce-motion helper, haptic feedback, reduce-transparency + increase-contrast, and voice-control input labels.
validations_passed: python3 automation/supervisor/run_next.py --dry-run (reports correctly that the next queued slice is blocked by this audit slice's own dirty paths)
scope_observed_from: owlory_xcode/Owlory/**/*.swift (motion, transparency, color, haptic, switch/voice/rotor pattern scan)
owlory_xcode/Owlory/Resources/en.lproj/Localizable.strings (already-localized accessibility hint strings that the follow-up slices can repurpose as accessibilityAction names)
gaps_named_for_follow_up: Color-only severity tint at TodayView.swift:1243 (finding M08) needs trace to its consumer to confirm whether shape/text are paired or if color alone signals severity.
On-device behavior under the actual user settings (Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency, Switch Control dwell timing, Voice Control command recognition) has not been verified.
Localized `*.voicecontrol.label.*` namespace for the Voice Control input labels slice does not yet exist; that slice will introduce it across all 19 locales.
not_claimed: Every animation, transparency, or color usage has been inventoried. The findings name the highest-traffic surfaces; smaller decorative effects may exist outside this scan.
Every swipeActions site has been traced to confirm whether the same action is also reachable via a button. Two sites might already have a tappable equivalent.
Switch Control or Voice Control have been physical-device tested. Findings are static-scan + HIG-pattern-based.
Reduce Motion / Reduce Transparency / Increase Contrast have been verified by toggling the actual user setting on a device.
residual_risks: If a future view adopts one of the four motion/transparency/contrast/color flags inline without going through a shared helper, the codebase will have inconsistent coverage. Recommendation: the reduce-motion-helper slice should set the helper-vs-inline convention.

The 12 swipeActions sites named in S01-S04 are reachable via swipe only as of today. Until the swipe-actions-as-accessibility-actions slice lands, Switch Control and VoiceOver users genuinely cannot reach those actions without an external workaround.
recommended_next_reason: Lowest priority among queued fix slices (76); largest concrete user-impact gap (12 actions invisible to Switch Control + VoiceOver today); existing localized strings already provide the action names so no localization fan-out.
repo_clean_status: scope-dirty (5 files staged for the audit slice; supervisor dry-run correctly flagged this against the NEXT slice scope)
